message:
feat(bench): add mux_pool_scaling benchmark for connection pool sizing

Add a new criterion benchmark (mux_pool_scaling) that measures mux
server throughput under varying pool sizes, exercising the vendored
connection pool with realistic concurrent pane allocation workloads.

Changes:
- New benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs: parameterized benchmark sweeping
  pool sizes from 4 to 128, measuring allocation throughput and
  contention overhead under concurrent access (314 lines)
- Cargo.toml: register [[bench]] entry with required "vendored" feature
- wal_throughput.rs: minor formatting alignment in existing benchmark

This benchmark supports data-driven pool sizing decisions for
large-scale swarm deployments where mux connection counts can
exceed 500 concurrent sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -170,6 +170,11 @@ harness = false
 name = "pool_benchmark"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "mux_pool_scaling"
+harness = false
+required-features = ["vendored"]
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "pdu_pipelining"
 harness = false
